package handler

import (
	"errors"
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for validator_outside_validation_package.
// Rule intent: Reusable validators that are scattered across handlers instead of
// living in a dedicated validation helper package.

// UserValidator is defined in the handler package instead of a validation package.
type UserValidator struct{}

func (v *UserValidator) ValidateName(name string) error {
	if name == "" {
		return errors.New("name is required")
	}
	if len(name) > 100 {
		return errors.New("name must be less than 100 characters")
	}
	return nil
}

func (v *UserValidator) ValidateEmail(email string) error {
	if email == "" {
		return errors.New("email is required")
	}
	if len(email) > 254 {
		return errors.New("email must be less than 254 characters")
	}
	return nil
}

// OrderValidator is also defined in the handler package — another validator outside validation package.
type OrderValidator struct{}

func (v *OrderValidator) ValidateAmount(amount float64) error {
	if amount <= 0 {
		return errors.New("amount must be positive")
	}
	if amount > 1000000 {
		return errors.New("amount exceeds maximum")
	}
	return nil
}

func CreateUserHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	validator := &UserValidator{}
	name := c.PostForm("name")
	email := c.PostForm("email")

	if err := validator.ValidateName(name); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}
	if err := validator.ValidateEmail(email); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"message": "user created"})
}
